'''Rainboh''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="daa" /> located in the [[Rainboh system/Legends|Rainboh system]] of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim's]] [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]]. Positioned among the [[Hapan Rim Worlds]], Rainboh could be reached from the [[Great Rim Route]] or the [[Rynmar Trail]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] via the planet [[Rynmar]] itself.<ref name="Atlas" />

The planet had rocky terrain, which appeared in several different [[Color/Legends|colors]]. Bands of purple, green, red, brown, and blue rocks made up the planet's surface, and rainbow-colored [[Water/Legends|raindrops]] fell on the world. There was at least one raised surface on Rainboh, and the planet's skyline featured one rounded, four-colored [[Satellite/Legends|satellite]].<ref name="daa" />

Rainboh was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the [[Jeby]], a three-headed, four-legged, tentacled-and-spotted [[Species/Legends|species]]. When a Jeby laughed boisterously, it shed its pink spots, littering the surface of Rainboh. However, the creature would then suck the spots back up onto its body.<ref name="daa" />

==Behind the scenes==
Rainboh was created by seven-year-old ''[[Star Wars]]'' fan [[Paul Rice]] for the backstory of his Jeby entry in the "[[Design An Alien]]" contest, which was held through the ''[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine]]''. Rice's submission was part of a school art project for which his first-grade teacher had selected the "Design An Alien" contest for students to complete.  Rice's entry was published in the magazine's [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|seventh issue]] in [[April]] [[1996]] under the "Honorable Mentions – Under 16" category.<ref name="daa" />

[[Leland Chee]], keeper of the ''Star Wars'' [[Holocron continuity database]], stated in [[2006]] that published content from the "Design An Alien" contest was [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the "Design an Alien" contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon? [...] Yes, though any text discrepencies with other sources would likely to superceded by the other source.}}</ref> ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', a reference book published in [[2009]], placed the planet grid square O-9.<ref name="Atlas" />
